---
title = "Getting Started"
---

## Documents

A *Calepin* notebook is a simple Markdown document with some added features: TOML front matter, executable code chunks, citations, cross-references, and Jinja templating. It looks a lot like a Quarto `.qmd` file, except that the preamble is specified in TOML rather than YAML (this may change before the 1.0 release).

Here is a minimal example:

````markdown
---
title = "My First Notebook"
date = "today"
bibliography = "references.bib"

[toc]
enabled = true
---

## Introduction

This is a *Calepin* document. Code chunks are executed and their
output is included in the rendered document.

```{r}
summary(mtcars[, 1:4])
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd
pd.DataFrame({"x": [1, 2, 3], "y": [4, 5, 6]})
```

See @knuth1984 for details.
````

Render it with:

```bash
calepin render notebook.qmd
```

## Commands

*Calepin* has seven main commands:

- `render`: produce output files.
- `preview`: start a live-reloading server.
- `new`: scaffold projects and generate shell completions.
- `theme`: apply or list themes.
- `info`: show available CSL styles and syntax themes.
- `man`: extract package documentation as `.qmd` files.
- `flush`: clean up caches and build artefacts.

Run `calepin --help` or `calepin <command> --help` for full option details.

### render

```bash
# Render a document to HTML (default)
calepin render document.qmd

# Choose an output format
calepin render document.qmd -t latex     # -> document.tex
calepin render document.qmd -t typst     # -> document.typ
calepin render document.qmd -t markdown  # -> document.md

# Render to multiple formats at once
calepin render document.qmd -t html,latex,typst

# Specify an output path (format inferred from extension)
calepin render document.qmd -o paper.tex

# Compile to PDF via LaTeX or Typst
calepin render document.qmd -t pdf
calepin render document.qmd -t pdf --writer typst

# Override metadata from the command line
calepin render document.qmd -s toc=true
calepin render document.qmd -s title="Draft" number-sections=true
calepin render document.qmd -s bibliography=refs.bib fig-width=6

# Quiet mode
calepin render document.qmd -q
```

### preview

```bash
# Live preview with hot reload
calepin preview document.qmd
calepin preview document.qmd --port 8080

# Preview a website project
calepin preview my_website/
```

### new

Scaffold new projects or generate shell completions.

```bash
# Scaffold a new project
calepin new notebook
calepin new website
calepin new book

# Generate shell completions
calepin new completions bash
calepin new completions zsh
calepin new completions fish
```

A scaffolded website includes a `_calepin/config.toml` with navbar, sidebar, and post-processing configuration, plus example pages in several directories.

### theme

Themes control the look of your website or document. A theme is a collection of partials and assets that override the built-in defaults.

```bash
# List available themes
calepin theme list

# Preview what a theme would change (dry run)
calepin theme show mytheme my_website/

# Apply a theme
calepin theme apply mytheme my_website/
```

### extra

Query built-in resources.

```bash
# List available CSL citation styles
calepin extra csl

# List available syntax highlighting themes
calepin extra themes
```

### man

Extract package documentation as `.qmd` files, ready for rendering or inclusion in a website.

```bash
# Extract R package documentation
calepin man r ggplot2
calepin man r ./path/to/package

# Extract Python package documentation
calepin man python numpy
```

### flush

```bash
# Delete cache and build artefacts
calepin flush
calepin flush -y              # skip confirmation
calepin flush --cache          # cache only
calepin flush --files          # generated files only
calepin flush --compilation    # LaTeX artefacts only (.aux, .log, etc.)
calepin flush path/to/dir      # flush a specific directory
```
